headline: AI trade could power higher in H2
detail: The article argues it wouldn't take much for the AI trade to surge again in the second half of the year. This is directly relevant to your book - CRWD, SMH, SOXL, QQQ, and QQQI all benefit from renewed AI momentum. If institutions rotate back in, your concentrated tech positioning works in your favor.
rec: No specific trade yet - but watch CRWD for the $740-744 scanner pullback entry if AI momentum builds. That setup has a Grade A bias and a defined stop at $731.72. Worth having the order ready, not placed blindly.

headline: Inflation fears called overblown
detail: If inflation fears are overstated, the market reduces expectations for further rate hikes. Lower-for-longer rates are bullish for growth and tech valuations - exactly your portfolio. This supports holding your QQQ, SMH, and CRWD positions without panic.
rec: No trade needed here. This headline reinforces holding your current tech names rather than trimming. Your stops are set - let the positions run unless those levels break.

headline: Defense spending surge drives drone stock +21%
detail: AeroVironment's massive single-day move shows real money flowing into defense. Trump's large defense budget headline adds fuel. You have no direct defense exposure, but this risk-on sentiment in high-growth sectors can spill into tech and chip names.
rec: No direct trade for your book. If you wanted defense exposure, RTX is on the scanner at entry $187.43-$187.91, stop $186.55 - tight risk, Grade A bias - but it's a new sector for you and adding margin in an unfamiliar name needs careful thought.

headline: Oil set for steep monthly drop
detail: Falling oil prices reduce input costs broadly and ease inflation pressure, which is a secondary positive for tech. More directly, ERY (inverse oil ETF) is on the scanner watchlist - but oil is already dropping, meaning the ERY long thesis would be fading, not building.
rec: Skip ERY - if oil is already falling sharply, chasing an inverse ETF after the move is poor risk/reward. No action warranted here for your portfolio.
